ksa_text: Knowledge of data remediation tools and techniques, including data cleansing, standardization, deduplication, enrichment, imputation approaches, and quality improvement workflows for addressing identified data issues.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting reviews of systems.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of performance tuning tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of performance tuning tools and techniques
Skill in conducting reviews of systems.
Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Quality falls outside the primary competency scope of the AI CoE Leader. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Data Quality truly out-of-scope for the AI CoE Leader? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Data Quality as a core or recommended competency for the AI CoE Leader. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Data Quality falls outside the established competency profile for the AI CoE Leader. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Data Quality is outside the competency scope of the AI CoE Leader. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
